danger_signals.visual_form: Distinct unit sprites with enemy faction coloring
Exhausted resource nodes becoming visually barren
danger_signals.meaning: Economic vulnerability and supply chain collapse risk

danger_signals.visual_form: Structures rendered in fire or rubble state
danger_signals.meaning: Active combat damage or settlement under attack

danger_signals.visual_form: Empty settlement buildings with minimal serf sprites visible
danger_signals.meaning: Economic collapse or insufficient labor for production chains

safety_signals.visual_form: Serfs moving between buildings, goods flowing through networks
safety_signals.meaning: Healthy economy and functioning supply networks

safety_signals.visual_form: Increasing number of serf entities in settlement areas
safety_signals.meaning: Sufficient resources and housing allowing population growth

safety_signals.visual_form: Military buildings and watchtowers in player faction colors
Goods accumulated near storage and distribution buildings

faction_indicators.color_identity: Primary faction color scheme (specific colors vary by campaign)
faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Enemy units rendered in contrasting faction colors to distinguish from player forces
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Buildings bearing faction color scheme; roads and infrastructure marked with player faction identifier

faction_indicators.color_identity: Distinct alternative color palettes for each opponent
faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Unit sprites with faction-specific armor/uniform coloring and shields
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Opponent settlements use faction colors; territorial markers indicate enemy zone control

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Lush green terrain, minimal military structures, emphasis on civilian buildings (housing, granaries, farms)
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Serfs focus on production and resource gathering; minimal military unit movement; peaceful trade routes established

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Dense settlement with many buildings; visible resource flows; populated streets with active serf traffic
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Complex production chains operating; increased serf density; advanced technology structures becoming visible (workshops, mills)

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Construction of barracks, watchtowers, and defensive walls; military unit icons appear on map; fortress structures prominent
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Serfs divert to military training buildings; armed units gather near borders; reduced civilian activity in threatened zones

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Smoke/fire visual effects near battle areas; destroyed building remnants; unit clash indicators; damaged terrain
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Military units engage in combat animations; defending units concentrate near fortifications; production halts in conflict zones

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Visible gaps in production buildings; reduced serf movement; dimmed or depleted resource node markers
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Serf queues form at distribution points; unemployment increases; construction stalls; rationing behavior evident

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Widespread building destruction; abandoned structures; minimal serf presence; faction color/flags removed or grayed out
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Serfs flee or disappear; no production activity; military units dispersed or defeated; infrastructure crumbles

environmental_storytelling.example: Early settlement areas show simple housing and farms; more advanced zones display workshops, breweries, and specialized production facilities, indicating technological advancement and economic investment over time
Faction-colored roads, flags on buildings, and boundary markers visually delineate controlled territory and indicate political boundaries between player and rival factions
High density of serf sprites around granaries and distribution centers during food shortage indicates famine conditions; clustering near military buildings shows military mobilization
Burned buildings, rubble, and damaged infrastructure serve as visual record of previous conflicts and territorial disputes
Depleted mine or forest sites become visually barren, telling story of over-exploitation and economic pressure on settlement
Presence of watchtowers, walls, and military buildings signals faction military capability and defensive posture to other factions and player

entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: Move between buildings in defined routes; carry goods; perform work animations at assigned stations
Stand stationary or wander aimlessly near housing; congregate at distribution centers; indicate economic problems
Move along borders or defensive positions; rotate to scan territory; remain stationed at military structures
Charge toward enemy units; clash animations with opposing forces; retreat behavior when outnumbered or losing
Serf activity visible; normal production building operations; no military mobilization; isolated from player territory
Military units gather and march toward player territory; fortifications reinforced; serf activity reduced in favor of military focus

notes: The Settlers (1993) is a foundational city-building real-time strategy game that uses primarily isometric or overhead perspective to communicate world-state through visual density, color-coding, and entity behavior. The game lacks explicit narrative dialogue but conveys political situations through faction representation, settlement positioning, and military structure visibility. Key manifestation layer elements include: faction color differentiation for quick player identification, serf behavior as economic health indicator, and environmental destruction as conflict record. The supply-demand economic system is visually communicated through visible production chains and resource flow animations.
sources_used: wikipedia - The Settlers series overview and gameplay mechanics
